title: ledum decumbens
intro: Ledum decumbens, also known as Labrador tea, is a type of evergreen shrub native to North America, particularly in the boreal forests of Canada and Alaska. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly by the indigenous peoples of North America, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Ledum decumbens include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of Ledum decumbens include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote relaxation, ease congestion, and support immune function.
